text: {"overallConclusion":"Infantino has breached political neutrality and may be corrupt.","inferences":[{"type":"evidence-for","premiseIndexes":[0],"conclusionIndex":-1,"warrant":"A formal complaint alleging rule violations provides evidence that the accused may have engaged in misconduct.","strength":"moderate","defeaters":["If the complaint is proven unfounded","If an independent investigation clears Infantino"]},{"type":"rebuts","premiseIndexes":[1],"conclusionIndex":0,"warrant":"A direct statement of non‑interference contradicts the implication that Infantino breached neutrality.","strength":"moderate","defeaters":["If other evidence shows he did influence the decision","If his statement is deemed unreliable"]},{"type":"undercuts","premiseIndexes":[4],"conclusionIndex":0,"warrant":"The autonomy of FIFA's judicial committees limits the president's capacity to intervene in disciplinary matters.","strength":"weak","defeaters":["If committees are shown to be subject to presidential influence","If autonomy is only formal"]},{"type":"explains","premiseIndexes":[5],"conclusionIndex":-1,"warrant":"IOC rules state that members who violate political neutrality can be expelled, explaining the seriousness of the alleged breach.","strength":"strong","defeaters":["If IOC chooses not to enforce the rule","If the alleged breach does not meet the threshold for expulsion"]},{"type":"presupposes","premiseIndexes":[3],"conclusionIndex":4,"warrant":"The principle that disciplinary decisions cannot be appealed assumes the independence of the adjudicating body.","strength":"weak","defeaters":["If appeals mechanisms exist in practice","If the rule is overridden by higher authority"]}]}
